expectations.text: Decided the marketing skill applies and used it
expectations.evidence: trigger_decision.json confirms triggered=true, citing cold email copywriting as explicitly covered by the marketing skill.

expectations.text: Subject line is present and under ~50 characters
expectations.evidence: Three subject options are provided, but option C ('Your best customers are about to leave — you just don't know which ones') is approximately 70 characters, well over ~50.

expectations.text: Includes preview/preheader text complementary to the subject
expectations.evidence: Preview text 'Most founders find out too late. Here's the early warning system.' is explicitly labeled and directly complements subject line A.

expectations.text: Opening is relevance-first (no generic throat-clearing like 'I hope this finds you well')
expectations.evidence: The email body opens with 'You already did the hard part — you got them to pay.' which immediately addresses the reader's situation with no generic preamble.

expectations.text: Body is one idea and under ~120 words
expectations.evidence: The email body from the opening through the CTA is approximately 87 words and stays focused on the single idea of detecting pre-churn signals.

expectations.text: Has exactly one low-friction CTA (yes/no or permission ask), not a 30-minute meeting demand
expectations.evidence: 'Worth a 15-minute look?' is framed as a yes/no question and requests a 15-minute slot, which is not a 30-minute meeting demand.

expectations.text: States assumptions inline rather than blocking with questions (scoped/low-stakes task)
expectations.evidence: The response opens with an explicit assumptions block (B2B SaaS, early-to-growth-stage founders, behavioral signals, no proof metrics) before writing the email.
